dialogue_text: That must be the Emerald City,
source_excerpt: "That must be the Emerald City," said Dorothy.

dialogue_text: What do you wish in the Emerald City?
source_excerpt: When he saw Dorothy and her companions the man asked, "What do you wish in the Emerald City?"

speaker.speaker_label: When he saw Dorothy and her companions the man
speaker.notes: Matched speaker phrase 'When he saw Dorothy and her companions the man' to 'dorothy'.

dialogue_text: We came here to see the Great Oz,
source_excerpt: "We came here to see the Great Oz," said Dorothy.

dialogue_text: It has been many years since anyone asked me to see Oz,

dialogue_text: He is powerful and terrible, and if you come on an idle or foolish errand to bother the wise reflections of the Great Wizard, he might be angry and destroy you all in an instant.

dialogue_text: But it is not a foolish errand, nor an idle one,
source_excerpt: "But it is not a foolish errand, nor an idle one," replied the Scarecrow; "it is important. And we have been told that Oz is a good Wizard."

source_excerpt: "But it is not a foolish errand, nor an idle one," replied the Scarecrow; "it is important. And we have been told that Oz is a good Wizard."

dialogue_text: and he rules the Emerald City wisely and well. But to those who are not honest, or who approach him from curiosity, he is most terrible, and few have ever dared ask to see his face. I am the Guardian of the Gates, and since you demand to see the Great Oz I must take you to his Palace. But first you must put on the spectacles.

dialogue_text: Because if you did not wear spectacles the brightness and glory of the Emerald City would blind you. Even those who live in the City must wear spectacles night and day. They are all locked on, for Oz so ordered it when the City was first built, and I have the only key that will unlock them.
